title: Multiple-choice cloze
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: Although humans have long attempted to understand the roots of creativity, recent research in cognitive science suggests that the process is far less linear than once believed. Early theories (0) creativity as a rare talent possessed by only a select few, but modern studies indicate that it emerges from a dynamic interplay of memory, emotion and environmental cues.||One of the most intriguing findings is that creativity often arises when the mind is allowed to (1) freely rather than focus narrowly on a single task. This mental drift can (2) unexpected associations, enabling individuals to generate novel ideas. However, such spontaneity does not (3) the importance of deliberate practice. In fact, experts argue that structured skill development can (4) the raw material needed for creative breakthroughs.||Another key factor is exposure to diverse experiences. People who regularly (5) themselves to unfamiliar situations tend to develop more flexible thinking patterns. Yet, diversity alone is not enough; individuals must also learn to (6) insights from different domains.||Ultimately, creativity appears to flourish when people strike a balance between disciplined effort and open-ended exploration. Understanding this balance may help educators (7) learning environments that nurture originality while still maintaining academic rigour. As research continues to evolve, scientists hope to (8) deeper into the mechanisms that shape human imagination.

example.explanation: “Regarded creativity as…” is the standard collocation. The others do not fit the meaning of considering something in a certain way.

questions.explanation: The intended answer is 'wander freely'. He has moved to option B. The others do not express mental wandering.
The correct one is 'prompt unexpected associations', now in C. The others do not form natural collocations with 'associations'.

The correct one is 'integrate insights', now in B. The others do not fit the meaning of combining knowledge.
